extra.reasoning:
The property is VIOLATED. The witness shows the "after" state (focal_weakness=true). Compare with the "before" state that differs only in focal_weakness=false.

Enum order low-to-high: self_care < routine < urgent < emergent.

BEFORE (rash=T, severe_headache=T, chest_pain=T, focal_weakness=FALSE, vision_loss=F):
- r1 chest_pain -> urgent (pri 2) matches
- r2 rash -> urgent (pri 2) matches
- r3 requires focal_weakness=true -> does NOT match
- r4 vision_loss=F -> no match
- r5 severe_headache -> emergent (pri 3) matches
Highest-priority match is r5 (pri 3) => acuity = emergent.

AFTER (same but focal_weakness=TRUE):
- r1 -> urgent (pri 2) matches
- r2 -> urgent (pri 2) matches
- r3 (rash AND severe_headache AND chest_pain AND focal_weakness all true) -> self_care (pri 100) matches
- r5 -> emergent (pri 3) matches
Highest-priority match is r3 (pri 100) => acuity = self_care.

Setting focal_weakness true changed acuity from emergent (highest) to self_care (lowest) - a strict decrease. The culprit is r3, the only rule referencing focal_weakness; it carries the dominating priority 100 and assigns the lowest acuity (self_care), overriding the emergent verdict from r5. This directly contradicts the monotonicity property that setting focal_weakness true never lowers acuity.
